prompt:
Our deploy scripts have zero tests because the CI runner image is frozen — no bats, no shunit2, no adding packages. Fine: I want our own micro framework, one sourceable file, pure bash 5, no external commands. `microtap.sh` in the repo root. Sourcing it must be silent (it only defines things; nothing runs, nothing prints). API, exactly this: - `mt_test ` registers a test (display names can contain spaces; functions run in registration order). - Assert helpers usable inside test functions, each returning 0 on pass and 1 on fail, and on failure printing ONE diagnostic line to stdout immediately (so it lands before that test's result line) while marking the current test failed — the test function keeps running, later asserts still execute and may add their own diagnostics: - `assert_eq ` -> diagnostic `# assert_eq failed: expected '' actual ''` - `assert_ne ` -> `# assert_ne failed: both ''` - `assert_fail ` runs the command, passes iff it exits nonzero -> `# assert_fail failed: command succeeded` - `assert_out ` captures the command's stdout the way `$(...)` does (trailing newlines dropped) and compares; the command's exit status is irrelevant -> `# assert_out failed: expected '' actual ''` - Hooks: if a function named `setup` exists it runs before EVERY test, in the same shell as the test (variables it sets are visible to the test). If `teardown` exists it runs after EVERY test — including tests that failed. - `mt_run` executes everything: first the plan line `1..`, then per test either `ok - ` or `not ok - ` (1-based, registration order), then a final `# pass

fail ` line. A test fails iff at least one of its asserts failed; a test whose asserts all pass is ok even if some command it observed had a nonzero status. mt_run RETURNS 1 if any test failed and 0 otherwise — it must not exit the caller (people call it from bigger scripts and inspect $?), so a suite script ending in `mt_run` naturally exits with the suite status. Nothing else may appear on stdout — the output IS the interface; our CI annotator parses it byte by byte. Acceptance: `bash test_microtap.sh` from the workspace root. Fair warning, it's the framework tested the honest way — the harness writes real sample suites (an all-green one, one with a mid-test failure, one proving teardown still runs after a failure) and diffs the TAP output byte-exactly, using its own plain comparisons rather than trusting your asserts. Don't modify the test file.
